title: service-accounts — settings
description: service-accounts exposes NO tenant-configurable settings. Every knob (rotation TTL, warning window, dormant threshold, TTL bounds, rate-limit thresholds) is a platform-wide policy owned by `service-accounts.php` config. Tenants cannot loosen any of these settings for their own SAs — the compliance floors (mandatory rotation, mandatory audit) apply uniformly. This file registers the `service-accounts` scope-consumer namespace so future keys land under a consistent prefix.

namespace_registration.defaultValueFactory: Delegates to config('service-accounts.*') when the tenant hasn't overridden. Placeholder — no tenant-visible settings are exposed today.
namespace_registration.validator: Enforces the mandatory-rotation floor (a tenant cannot extend expires_at beyond `service-accounts.rotation.default_secret_ttl_days` × 2 = 180d), the audit-required floor (audit middleware cannot be disabled), and the entitlement-gated ceilings (extended TTL requires enterprise entitlement).

notes: service-accounts is a substrate module. Every configuration knob is platform-wide — no tenant customisation of security controls.
The one legitimate per-SA override is `expires_at` at creation (bounded by config; enterprise entitlement removes the ceiling). That's a WRITE-time setting, not a scope-substrate value.

The `service_accounts_ip_allowlist` entitlement's per-SA metadata list IS per-row settings-adjacent, but it's stored in the row's `metadata` JSONB, not in the settings substrate. Rationale: the allowlist is enforcement data on the hot path (audit middleware reads it), not configuration.
